capability_objective: For 2026-11-25, I need independent availability results for “Theft claim” (West Office) and “Windshield claim” (North Office). Send both checks in the same tool-call action. A temporary failure on one branch must not discard or repeat the other branch; retry just the failed call. Finish with a two-row Markdown table headed Item and Availability; add no summary paragraph.
prompt:
For the insurance desk's 2026-11-25 planning, check availability for the exact item `Theft claim` at `West Office` and the exact item `Windshield claim` at `North Office`. Work only in the provided sandbox. Use the genuine executable `./claim-availability` through Pi's Bash tool, beginning with its built-in `--help` to discover the interface. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, its runtime evidence, or anything under `.harness`, and do not modify any supplied file.

The checks are independent. In the first availability-check action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, with one exact check in each call; do not put both checks in one shell command and do not launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both calls to return. If exactly one call reports a temporary retryable failure, keep the successful branch's returned availability and retry only the failed exact call in a later Pi Bash tool call. Do not repeat a successful check. Do not broaden either request, substitute another date, item, or office, or make any additional availability check. Base each result only on successful executable output.

Write the complete result to `availability.md` and create no other file. Its entire contents must be a newline-terminated Markdown table with exactly the columns `Item` and `Availability`, the standard separator `| --- | --- |`, and exactly two body rows in the requested order. Use the item text exactly as requested and the availability text exactly as returned by each successful check. Include one space on each side of every cell value. Add no heading, blank line, code fence, note, error discussion, or summary paragraph. After writing the file, return exactly the same table as the final response.
